replacement_target: later explicit repo-operating-contract checkpoint or replacement artifact only

current_program_context.series_role: workflow hardening and repo guidance that must not compete with playable-baseline work
current_program_context.highest_value_immediate_move: harden one canonical repo-local playtest path around session creation plus local loopback authority, then rerun FAST validation and a selected playtest smoke slice

why_canonical_now: CMakePresets.json defines aligned verify configure, build, and test presets
the ultra audit identifies the verify lane as the strongest current build baseline for playable-baseline work
the verify lane keeps tools, setup, launcher, game, and tests enabled inside the repo verification posture
required_when: compiled runtime, launcher, setup, server, client, or networking behavior is touched
the task needs fresh verified binaries under out/build/vs2026/verify/
verified_enough: docs-only or mirror-only work can stop at parsing, prose/mirror consistency, and git diff checks
Python or shell-path work should normally reach FAST validation plus directly relevant script-level checks
compiled or playtest-path work should reach verify configure/build plus the relevant runtime or test surface
must_not_assume: a successful configure alone is not a verified build
an existing out/build/vs2026/verify tree is not proof that current changes were rebuilt
local and release-check are valid presets but not the canonical default lane for current baseline assembly work

testx_companion.role: canonical XStack/TestX smoke companion when XStack tooling or validation surfaces are touched

ctest_companion.role: canonical compiled-test companion when the verify binary lane is implicated

role_split.FAST: repo-wide first pass and minimum honest default for non-trivial work
role_split.TestX: higher-signal Python/XStack harness for tooling, control, compat, session, and validation-adjacent changes
role_split.CTest: compiled/runtime harness for verify-built binaries and related smoke coverage

smoke_is_enough_when: the task is small and localized
the touched surface has a narrow validation target
no broader runtime or compiled behavior changed
stronger_validation_needed_when: the task changes build wiring, launcher/setup entrypoints, server or client runtime behavior, or session boot flow
the task changes control, compat, validation, or trust semantics across more than one narrow surface
the prompt explicitly requires STRICT, FULL, or broader execution

honest_status_language.verified: use only when the relevant command completed successfully
honest_status_language.partially_verified: use when only a subset completed
honest_status_language.attempted_but_blocked: use when a command failed or timed out due to a real blocker
honest_status_language.not_run: use when a command was not executed

default_posture: default MVP bundle and pack lock
Python/AppShell operator shells instead of native-wrapper-first assumptions

current_strongest_public_or_semi_public_.action: prepare the verify lane when compiled artifacts are implicated
prefer Python/AppShell shells as operator-facing surfaces
materialize the session under the canonical repo-local save tree
treat local loopback authority as the canonical runtime direction

current_truthfulness_rules: there is not yet one finished, stable, single public playtest command
the canonical path today is a target recipe, not a completed one-command operator flow
Codex must not invent a second official path because another wrapper exists

blocked_or_fragile.reason: boot flow remains coupled to canonical repo_root/saves/<save_id> layout

must_not_assume_yet: raw python server/server_main.py is not the canonical public playtest path
python -m server.server_main is not a safe canonical substitute
python tools/mvp/runtime_entry.py --local-singleplayer is transitional and not the canonical operator path
alternate save roots are not end-to-end supported just because session_create.py accepts --saves-root
non-loopback multiplayer, installed-mode assumptions, and native launcher/setup parity are not baseline-ready

rules: use session_create.py as the canonical session materialization entrypoint
start from the default bundle and pack lock unless the task explicitly requires a different profile family
do not silently change save-root expectations in docs, scripts, or prompts
if session_boot is used, point it at the canonical saves/<save_id>/session_spec.json layout and state the save-root coupling clearly

current_save_root_truth.why: sessionx.runner resolves the active save directory back through repo_root/saves/<save_id>
current_save_root_truth.codex_reporting_rule: describe alternate save-root support as blocked, not partially working

authoritative_directories.notes: canonical constitutional, planning, and XStack operating-law surfaces; for docs/planning use the task-named authoritative artifacts
authoritative semantic and contract law when touched
authoritative runtime, launcher, setup, loopback, and XStack assembly roots for the playable-baseline path
authoritative profile, lock, pack, and session authoring roots; data/packs remains ownership-sensitive and is not silently interchangeable with packs
authoritative validation and test roots always; release, repo, security, and updates are authoritative when that scope is explicitly in play

derived_or_generated_directories.notes: machine-readable mirrors that must align with canonical prose and must not overrule it
audit evidence and reporting; operationally important but not canonical law
validator-facing projections or advisory mirrors, not canonical semantic contract law
generated build, cache, report, and temporary outputs
built or runtime-state outputs; saves is the current canonical boot location for session instances but not an authored source root

avoid_touching_unless_explicitly_tasked: broad non-loopback multiplayer or external transport expansion
full AIDE runtime, daemon, compiler, adapter ecosystem, or platformization work
large renames or convergence passes not required to unblock the canonical repo-local playtest path
ownership rebinding across field versus fields, schema versus schemas, or packs versus data/packs
native launcher/setup shell completion work that does not directly unblock the baseline path
release/publication/archive/trust policy changes unrelated to the active task
doctrine rewrites under docs/canon/** or AGENTS.md without an explicit doctrine-facing prompt
promotion of tools/mvp/runtime_entry.py into the canonical public playtest path without an explicit task
wrapper proliferation that creates another official startup flow

exception_rule.allowed_when: a frozen surface contains a named blocker from the audit and the active prompt directly targets that blocker
constraints: do not widen into speculative cleanup

big_prompts.guardrail: must not become stealth implementation sprees

use_for: one vertical slice of the canonical playable-baseline path
one blocker plus immediate support docs or tests
a targeted integration repair spanning a small number of closely related roots
typical_scope: one blocker or one operator workflow
at most a handful of directly connected roots

use_for: one doc or JSON mirror update

too_broad_when: the task mixes baseline blockers with frontier expansion
the task mixes doctrine rewrite with runtime implementation
the task spans multiple independent blockers from the audit gap ledger
the task combines broad renaming, extraction, and baseline assembly work
the task cannot be validated honestly inside one bounded change set

code_rules: add or clarify docstrings when entrypoint behavior, path assumptions, contracts, or failure modes are not obvious
add short inline comments only where the logic would otherwise be hard to reconstruct quickly
explain boundary conditions, refusal paths, fallback limits, and save-root or repo-root assumptions clearly
document non-obvious operator expectations close to the enforcing code
avoid decorative, repetitive, or tutorial-style comments
docs_rules: keep documentation explicit about what is verified, likely, partial, blocked, or deferred
name real commands, presets, paths, blockers, and defaults
distinguish doctrine from implementation, implementation from integration, and integration from deferred extraction
update paired prose and machine-readable mirrors together when both are part of the contract surface

body_requirements: canonical rules or paths frozen or modified
validation run, skipped, blocked, or partial
next prompt or next engineering move enabled
general_rules: mention blockers, deferred work, and limitations honestly
keep unrelated dirty files out of the commit
commit at meaningful boundaries rather than batching unrelated work into one change

forbidden_operating_patterns: assuming documentation outranks live implementation when the authority order says otherwise
treating reports, mirrors, or generated outputs as canonical law
touching unrelated roots during baseline work because they look adjacent
inventing a second canonical playtest path
claiming validation success without running the relevant validation
treating experimental or transitional wrappers as canonical operator surfaces without evidence
silently promoting raw server/server_main.py or tools/mvp/runtime_entry.py into the public playtest path
competing with the playable-baseline path by expanding into large AIDE runtime or platform work
flattening verified, likely, partial, and blocked into one status label
silently rebinding ownership-sensitive roots because one side is easier to consume
downstream_prompts_enabled: playable-baseline assembly prompts that need one canonical operating rule set
future Codex runs that need stable build, validation, playtest, directory-authority, and task-sizing guidance
